name: packaging (Python Packaging Utilities)

agent_readiness.notes: Official PyPA library for PEP 440 version parsing and comparison. from packaging.version import Version. v = Version('2.1.0'). v.major, v.minor, v.micro. Version('1.0') < Version('2.0'). from packaging.specifiers import SpecifierSet. sp = SpecifierSet('>=1.0,<3.0'). '2.0' in sp. from packaging.requirements import Requirement. r = Requirement('requests>=2.28.0; python_version >= "3.8"'). r.name, r.specifier, r.marker. from packaging.markers import Marker. from packaging.tags import sys_tags. from packaging.utils import canonicalize_name, parse_wheel_filename. canonicalize_name('Requests') → 'requests'. Used internally by pip, setuptools, etc.

gotchas: PEP 440 pre-release versions (1.0a1, 1.0b2, 1.0rc1) sort before release — may surprise
LegacyVersion removed in packaging 22+ — non-PEP-440 versions raise InvalidVersion
Marker evaluation depends on runtime environment — test on target Python version
Tags module (packaging.tags) is platform-specific — behaves differently per OS
best_when: Parsing and comparing Python package versions (PEP 440)
Evaluating dependency specifiers and markers programmatically
Building package management tools, dependency resolvers, or CI checks
Canonicalizing package names (pip, PyPI normalization rules)
avoid_when: When you only need simple string comparison of semver (use semver library)
Non-Python version schemes — packaging is Python/PEP-440 specific
summary: packaging is the official PyPA library for PEP 440 version parsing, comparison, and requirement parsing. Version('2.1.0') < Version('3.0'). SpecifierSet('>=1.0,<3.0'). Requirement('requests>=2.28'). canonicalize_name(). Apache-2.0/BSD-2 by PyPA. Type-annotated. Used internally by pip and setuptools. Best for version comparison, dependency resolution, and package tooling. LegacyVersion removed in v22+.
